mnemonics: POINTER: Points Out Important Numbers To Extract Reference
keyPoints: A pointer stores a memory address rather than the value itself
The dereference operator (*) accesses the value at the stored memory address
Pointers must be declared with a specific data type (int*, char*, etc.)
commonMistakes: Dereferencing a NULL or uninitialized pointer (causes segmentation fault)
Forgetting to use the address-of operator (&) when assigning to a pointer
Confusing when to use the asterisk (*) for declaration vs. dereferencing
